summary: Pristine‑sounding audience tape captures a high‑energy 1982 Golden Hall show with a monster Let It Grow and the rare Franklin’s > Samson opener.
review: The recording delivers an immersive ‘you are there’ feeling, generally clear and crisp despite occasional sketchiness common to stealth audience tapes. Musically the concert is a standout – the first set shines with a soaring To Lay Me Down and a stellar China Cat > I Know You Rider, while the second set erupts with an 18‑minute Let It Grow, a flawless Franklin’s > Samson transition and powerful Playing in the Band. Garcia’s lead work, Brent Mydland’s solo jam and Bob Weir’s on‑stage banter add extra flavor to an already memorable night.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently strong praise for both performance (standout songs, unique set flow) and recording quality (crisp, immersive), with only minor complaints about occasional sketchiness.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and crisp with a strong ‘you‑are‑there’ vibe; some reviewers note occasional sketchy moments typical of stealth audience recordings.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds nicely with tight transitions; second set opens uniquely with Franklin’s > Samson and maintains high energy throughout, delivering several epic jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia ‘tears up’ Let It Grow, delivering powerful lead guitar work.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir adds a spoken “Pay Attention!” during Samson and contributes strong vocal presence.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland shines with an impressive solo jam that flows into the drums.
